---
title: "Planning Roadmap"
order: 1
execute:
  freeze: auto 
---

The roadmap is for anyone who needs to know about planned changes to the 
demand model. It contains high-level indicative areas of development that may
be subject to change in timing and/or scope as programme priorities shift.

This roadmap is updated quarterly.

### Last updated: June 2026

## Recently Completed 
(for full details see [model updates](https://connect.strategyunitwm.nhs.uk/nhp/project_information/project_plan_and_summary/model_updates.html))


- Refresh of health status adjustment distribution values following expert elicitation.
- Extend the available NHP model horizon years up to 2047.

## Working on Now

- Creating the 'engine' to allow modelled predicted hospital activity to be converted into estimated capacity requirements.
- Prioritised extensions to the current ["Explore opportunities to reduce hospital care"] application functionality.
- Inequalities adjustment functionality to allow demand predictions to assume inequality resolution per HRG by the horizon year
- Implementation of an updated waiting list adjustment module for the NHP demand model.

## Working on Next

- Development of robust piplelines for predicting future hospital activity at NHS Region level.
- In-house site-level modelling capabilities to allow finer control on hospital activity prediction for multi-site schemes.
- User interface development for ICB NHP demand modelling so users can submit model scenarios.
- Development of an open National model scenario library website for training , knowledge sharing and illustrative purposes.

## Exploring

- Improving the user experience for the NHP Project Information site and related communications.
- Updates to National expert-elicited values of TPMA assumptions based on new elicitation activity.
- Deprecate the current method of generalised additive models used in age-specific health estimation to something better supported.
- A tool to assist groups of people to input TPMA assumptions for a model scenario, support discussion and eventual agreement on a single assumption per TPMA for a given scenario.
- A tool to assist with National elicitation exercises.
